data.body:
[PASS 1 — T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification — review of epic 1f19 + children]

VERDICT: CLEAN for T1. No findings. The bright lines that trigger this overlay (security/auth — HMAC-signed verdicts; novel architectural patterns — content-addressed snapshot layout, delete-on-last-close reader safety, flock GC interlock; deep external integration — git plumbing) are all met with explicit prior-art grounding and justified novelty. Affirmations below.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies):

- [T1(a) prior art considered] STRONG. The epic Context explicitly grounds the design against a named prior-art set — "Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto" — covering the four relevant lineages: server-side git materialization (Gitaly, gitserver), content-addressed build/artifact caching (Bazel, ccache, Nix), faithful tree export (GitHub tarball API), and signed-attestation envelopes (in-toto). This is not a token name-drop: each maps to a concrete design decision downstream (e.g. content-addressed `<cache>/<sha>/` ← Bazel/ccache/Nix; in-toto Statement shape ← in-toto). location: epic 1f19 "Context" final sentence; S1 6d56 "What"; S4 30b3 "What".

- [T1(a) anti-reinvention] S1 (6d56) does NOT reinvent tree extraction: it explicitly rejects `git archive` with a grounded, accurate enumeration of its lossiness (drops `export-ignore` files, omits submodules, returns LFS pointer files, rewrites `export-subst`) and adopts the established `git read-tree` (into a `GIT_INDEX_FILE` temp index) + `git checkout-index --all --prefix=` idiom instead. That is the correct, well-trodden git-plumbing approach for a faithful committed-tree snapshot — properly chosen over the lossy convenience path, with the rationale stated. location: S1 6d56 "Why" + "What" (faithful tree materialization bullet).

- [T1(b) novelty justified vs established approach] The plan does not hand-wave its novel cross-cutting patterns: the epic's "Architecture decision record" section enumerates them (content-addressed snapshot layout, delete-on-last-close reader safety, flock GC interlock, read-tree+checkout-index over git archive, manifest-channel SHA binding) AND names the REJECTED alternatives (PID+heartbeat lease; payload-schema SHA binding) to be captured in an ADR "so future maintainers see the decision + rejected alternatives." Parent AC8 makes the ADR a closure gate. This is exactly the anti-repackaging discipline T1 asks for. location: epic 1f19 "Architecture decision record" + AC "An ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives (PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding)"; S2 70c7 / S4 30b3 ADR-authoring ACs.

- [T1(b) Rule-of-Three / YAGNI applied] S4b (e308) explicitly runs the Rule-of-Three/YAGNI check before introducing the shared ref-resolution boundary: "there are exactly two consumers today (plan-review hashing + claim-gate re-check); the consolidation is justified by the divergence risk above and is de-risked by an explicit back-out." Consolidating at two consumers is defensible here because the justification is a correctness risk (whole-HEAD vs pinned-SHA divergence re-introducing the staleness false-positive), not speculative generality — and it is paired with a stated reversible back-out. Exemplary handling. location: S4b e308 "What" (Rule-of-Three/YAGNI bullet) + "Back-out" bullet.

- [T1(c) capability assertions resolved] The one load-bearing external-capability assertion — that fetching an arbitrary SHA requires the remote's `uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant` (a server-side config the plan cannot guarantee on, e.g., GitHub) — is RESOLVED at the plan level: S1 documents the prerequisite AND supplies a fallback ("or fetch a containing ref then resolve") plus a `--filter=blob:none` preference over deep shallow refetch. The HMAC-now / DSSE-asymmetric-transparency-log-later posture is likewise framed as a deliberate, documented limitation (S4 shapes the manifest pin as an in-toto Statement so the envelope swap is not a rewrite; limits documented in S6), not an unverified claim. location: S1 6d56 "What" (Fetch prerequisites bullet); S4 30b3 "What" (in-toto envelope-swap bullet).

Net: this plan is, if anything, a model for the T1 overlay — prior art is named and load-bearing, every novel pattern is justified against a rejected alternative and slated for an ADR, the one consolidation passes an explicit Rule-of-Three gate, and the external git-capability dependency is acknowledged with a fallback. No T1 finding.
